prompt: Using ProteinMPNN with multi-state design, generate sequences for human adenylate kinase (ADK, PDB 4AKE open; PDB 1AKE closed) that are computationally predicted to populate both conformational states with near-equal probability as a model system for studying enzyme conformational selection. outline a computational approach covering: (1) multi-state ProteinMPNN run using both open and closed backbones simultaneously, (2) negative design against sequences that strongly favour only one state (by energy gap), (3) MD simulation of top-10 variants to assess free-energy difference between states, (4) experimental validation by FRET-labelled smFRET assay. Discuss implications for understanding catalytic cycle and drug resistance in kinases.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (4AKE, 1AKE); target: Adenylate kinase; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0
